posts.author: pllgevdk (Lennard Larsen)
posts.cookedHtml:
What
An input field that is validated to a regex expression, and has a minimum and maximum length.

Why
Sometimes you need an input to validate to a specific set of criteria fx. alfanum between 4 and 12 characters, or an 8 digit number.

Having a Regex input type would solve this problem.

posts.author: Huskydog (F)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to stress how important this feature is for us.

We have endless use cases where Regex would make all the difference – serial numbers, MAC addresses, IPs, Darwin numbers, article numbers, ERP references… you name it. Without input validation, people enter all kinds of “creative” formats, and workarounds appear faster than you can blink. This doesn’t just create messy data – it makes monday.com less reliable for critical business processes.

Regex is not a niche request. Across industries, it’s a standard tool for ensuring clean, consistent, and searchable data. Developers use it to validate inputs and clean logs, marketers use it in Google Analytics, system admins rely on it for parsing configs – it’s proven and universal.

Why it matters to monday.com:

  • Customer trust – if data stays clean, teams can rely on monday for core business processes without constant manual fixing.

  • Stickiness – once companies structure their key data here, it’s much harder to move away.

  • Scalability – Regex enables complex, enterprise-level use cases without forcing people into error-prone workarounds.

A simple example: a serial number format MMYY-NNNC

  • MM = month

  • YY = year

  • NNN = running number

  • C = check character

Regex pattern:

^(0[1-9]|1[0-2])\d{2}-\d{3}[A-Z]$

Valid examples (accepted):

  • 0925-123A → September 2025, running number 123, check “A”

  • 0119-007C → January 2019, running number 007, check “C”

Invalid examples (blocked):

  • 2025-123A → wrong format, year is not two digits

  • 1325-123A → invalid month (13)

  • 0925-12A → missing one digit in the running number

  • 0925-1234 → missing check character

  • 0925-123$ → special character not allowed

For us, Regex is not “nice to have” – it’s a foundation for data quality, automation, and reliable reporting. Adding it would massively increase the value of monday.com in real-world operations.
